articles.title: TEAMS ARE ALREADY ADJUSTING ROADMAPS
articles.summary: Agentic AI is quickly moving from experiment to roadmap priority. In a survey of 1,000+ developers and product leaders, most said agentic capabilities will soon be a baseline expectation across modern software.

articles.title: HOW GENERATIVE UI CUT OUR DEVELOPMENT TIME FROM MONTHS TO WEEKS
articles.summary: Generative UI builds interfaces dynamically using approved components, contextual data, and a trained language model instead of fixed layouts. When supported by strong design systems and guardrails, it can dramatically speed development and tailor software to each user's situation.

articles.title: FORWARD DEPLOYED DESIGNER
articles.summary: AI enables designers to embed with teams and rapidly prototype real solutions, creating a new “Forward Deployed Designer” role. Small squads that combine design, engineering, and research can dramatically accelerate learning and product discovery.

articles.title: AI MADE RELATIONSHIPS, NOT FEATURES, THE LAST DEFENSIBLE ADVANTAGE
articles.summary: As AI commoditizes features, competitive advantage shifts to the relationship products build with users. Context, history, and trust create switching costs that features alone no longer can.

articles.title: THE INTERFACE WAS THE PRODUCT
articles.summary: AI removes interface learning but also removes the natural discovery that shows users a product's value. The new challenge is making capabilities visible and surfacing the right value at the right moment.

articles.title: HOW GSK USES AI AGENTS TO TURN DATA INTO DECISIONS — NOT DASHBOARDS
articles.summary: BI tools were built to report. Not to act. GSK and Huel's data leaders join ThoughtSpot at a free virtual event to show how AI analytics agents are replacing static dashboards with answers you can actually use. Watch the Spotlight Series [10]. Also grab the Embedded AI Agents Ebook [9] for the full playbook.

articles.title: ORGANIZING PRODUCTIVE PLATFORM TEAMS
articles.summary: Platform systems reflect the structure of the organizations that build them. Effective platform teams reduce developer complexity by aligning teams to capabilities, defining clear interfaces, and evolving with the architecture.

articles.title: SUMMONING THE COURAGE TO FOCUS
articles.summary: Strategy requires the courage to focus by rejecting many good ideas. Teams must build confidence through testing assumptions and gathering evidence to prioritize what matters most.

articles.title: MACHINE LEARNING AT SCALE: MANAGING MORE THAN ONE MODEL IN PRODUCTION
articles.summary: At scale, machine learning shifts from optimizing a single model to managing many models reliably. The key is designing systems that stay available, fail safely, avoid data leakage, and include human oversight before models reach users.

articles.title: THE AI-ENABLED ORG IS A VENN DIAGRAM
articles.summary: AI-driven development increases overlap between product, engineering, and other roles rather than replacing them. The future likely belongs to small, highly collaborative teams where disciplines intersect to deliver products faster.

articles.title: INTERNAL PLATFORM TECHNICAL PRODUCT MANAGER (TPM) PATTERNS: PROS AND CONS
articles.summary: Organizations assign TPM responsibilities through dedicated TPMs, shared TPMs, Engineering Managers, or Staff engineers.

articles.title: THE SWORD OF DAMOCLES IN SOFTWARE
articles.summary: AI is accelerating software commoditization, allowing competitors to quickly replicate features and erode market leaders.
